title: Bloom Energy Signs $5bn Partnership With Brookfield to Deploy Fuel Cell Tech Across AI Data Centers
text: Bloom Energy and Brookfield announced a $5 billion strategic partnership for deploying fuel cell technology across AI data centers. The partnership establishes a powerful collaboration addressing the critical need for reliable, scalable power solutions in AI infrastructure buildout. Brookfield's investment demonstrates confidence in Bloom Energy's solid oxide fuel cell technology as the preferred solution for powering next-generation AI data centers. The partnership involves collaborative design and deployment of AI factories globally with planned facilities in multiple regions including Europe. The structure allows Brookfield to finance fuel cell deployments, shifting capital expenditure burden from data center operators and enabling faster deployment. This financing model is critical for hyperscalers and colocation providers managing massive infrastructure expansion needs. The partnership leverages Bloom's proven track record with over 1,000 installed systems globally and Brookfield's expertise in infrastructure investment and management. Together, the companies aim to build AI factories that tightly integrate compute, power, data center architecture, and capital to meet the rapidly growing demands of artificial intelligence deployment.
